Operation 'authenticateUser' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description is too vague and lacks critical details. It doesn't explain what inputs are expected (credentials), what is returned (tokens/session), or the side effects (session establishment). Added specifics about authentication flow, return values, error handling, and prerequisite nature for AI agents to understand when and how to use this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Authenticates a user by verifying credentials and establishing a session. Accepts user credentials as input and returns authentication tokens or session information upon successful verification. Failed authentication attempts are rejected with appropriate error responses. This operation is required before accessing protected resources.

message: Operation 'fetchUserWorkspaces' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Current description lacks detail about return structure and authentication requirements. Enhanced description specifies the return type (list of Workspace objects), references the Workspace schema, clarifies authentication requirement, and explains filtering logic (only user's workspaces). data.current_description: Lists workspaces the user participates in.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all workspaces associated with the authenticated user. Returns a list of Workspace objects containing workspace metadata including ID, name, and other relevant properties. This operation requires user authentication and returns only workspaces where the user has active participation or membership.

data.current_description: Retrieves users associated with a workspace.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all contacts (users) associated with a specific workspace. Accepts workspace identifier and optional filtering parameters to narrow results. Returns a list of Contact objects containing user information including ID, name, and contact details. Requires appropriate workspace access permissions. Supports pagination through the 4 available parameters.

data.current_description: Attaches a file to an existing note.
data.description_suggestion: Attaches a file to an existing note identified by its UUID. Accepts file data and metadata through the request body and associates it with the specified note. Returns a FileResponse object containing the file's metadata including ID, name, and storage information. The file is persisted and linked to the note, making it accessible when retrieving note details. Requires the note to exist and user to have write permissions.
